forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,44 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: fb80
+title: Cut/copy/paste shown on views & perspectives that don't support them — gate command availability through the plugin system, hide when unsupported
+---
+## What
+
+Views and perspectives expose cut/copy/paste commands that are not supported for their entity type. Today they show up anyway (and presumably no-op or error). They should NOT be displayed when the focused entity type doesn't support them.
+
+The gating must come from the **plugin system** — i.e. the command's availability/applicability for the focused entity type is declared/resolved by the plugin that owns the command (the same metadata-driven path that decides which commands apply to a scope), NOT a hardcoded React check that special-cases "view" / "perspective".
+
+## Expected
+
+- For a focused entity type that does not support cut/copy/paste (views, perspectives), those commands are simply absent from the command surface (palette / context menu / keybinding availability) — not shown-and-disabled, not shown-and-erroring.
+- For entity types that DO support them (tasks/cards), they continue to appear and work.
+- Availability is computed from plugin/command metadata for the focused scope, not branched on entity-type strings in the UI.
+
+## Design / investigate
+
+- This is a metadata-driven-UI concern: the UI is an interpreter of command metadata for the focused scope chain, never hardcoding entity logic in React. Reuse the existing command-applicability/scope-resolution path that already decides which commands render for a focused entity (the same machinery behind `{{entity.type}}` caption rendering and per-scope command lists).
+- Find where cut/copy/paste commands are declared: likely entity.yaml / a clipboard or edit command YAML, plus the paste path (PasteMatrix — internal vs external drag distinction already exists). Determine why they leak onto view/perspective scopes — are they declared at a too-broad scope, or is the UI listing all commands regardless of applicability?
+- The fix is to have the plugin/command declaration express which entity types support cut/copy/paste (capability/applicability), and the command surface filter by that — so unsupported entities never list them.
+- Confirm copy/cut/paste are genuinely unsupported for views & perspectives (the user says it's OK that they're unsupported — the ask is purely to stop showing them).
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] Cut/copy/paste do not appear on the command surface when a view or perspective is focused
+- [ ] Cut/copy/paste still appear and work for entity types that support them (tasks/cards)
+- [ ] Availability is resolved from plugin/command metadata for the focused scope — no hardcoded entity-type branch in React
+- [ ] No regression to internal-drag (task.move) vs external-drag (paste) dispatch separation
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] vitest red-first: with a view/perspective focused, the command surface (palette/menu) does NOT include cut/copy/paste
+- [ ] vitest: with a task/card focused, cut/copy/paste ARE present
+- [ ] command-metadata/applicability test proving the gate is driven by declared capability, not a UI string check
+- [ ] tsc + touched vitest green; relevant cargo nextest packages green if command YAML/declaration changes touch a Rust crate
+
+## Constraints
+- NO whole-workspace cargo build/clippy. Never touch .kanban/actors/wballard.jsonl.
+- Metadata-driven only — do not hardcode view/perspective special-casing in React (see metadata-driven-ui feedback). Cross-cutting commands live once in entity.yaml/ui.yaml; type-specific commands live in their noun's YAML — gate via applicability, don't re-list.
+
+## Workflow
+- /tdd — failing test first: focused view/perspective excludes cut/copy/paste from the command surface.
\ No newline at end of file

--- original
+++ modified
@@ -41,4 +41,23 @@
 - Metadata-driven only — do not hardcode view/perspective special-casing in React (see metadata-driven-ui feedback). Cross-cutting commands live once in entity.yaml/ui.yaml; type-specific commands live in their noun's YAML — gate via applicability, don't re-list.

\ No newline at end of file
+- /tdd — failing test first: focused view/perspective excludes cut/copy/paste from the command surface.
+
+## Review Findings (2026-06-13 14:53)
+
+Overall: the fix is well-designed and metadata-driven — the gate is a declared entity-type set (`applies_to`) interpreted by one code path (`CommandService::applies_to_focus` → `caption::focused_entity_type`), with NO hardcoded "view"/"perspective" branch in either Rust or React (verified by grep across `apps/kanban-app/ui/src`, `builtin/plugins/entity-commands`, `builtin/plugins/app-shell-commands`). `focused_entity_type` is now shared by caption rendering and the gate (two callers), so label and availability are decided by one rule. Tests are green and the red-green probe holds. No blockers. The findings below are a data-drift warning and two nits.
+
+### Warnings
+- [ ] `builtin/plugins/entity-commands/index.ts:72` + `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/clipboard_commands.rs:30` — The clipboard capability set is duplicated as TWO independent source-of-truth lists: TS `CLIPBOARD_ENTITY_TYPES` (list-time gate) and Rust `COPYABLE_ENTITY_TYPES` (dispatch-time `available()` gate). They currently agree exactly (`task, tag, column, board, actor, project, attachment`), but lockstep is enforced only by prose comments ("Mirrors…/Must stay in sync…") plus a hardcoded literal in `tests/integration/builtin_entity_commands_e2e.rs::assert_clipboard_applies_to` (which pins the TS-declared set against a third hand-maintained literal, not against the Rust list). Declared ⟺ enforced can silently drift: e.g. if Rust later drops `actor` from `COPYABLE_ENTITY_TYPES`, the command would still LIST for a focused actor (TS unchanged) but then no-op/fail at dispatch — re-introducing a shown-but-unsupported command, the exact class of bug this card fixes. Suggested fix: add a single drift-guard test asserting the TS-surfaced `applies_to` equals the Rust `COPYABLE_ENTITY_TYPES` (the e2e harness already loads the plugin and has the registered metadata + the Rust constant in scope), so the two lists cannot diverge without a red test.
+
+### Nits
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/src/caption.rs:56` (`INSPECTABLE_ENTITY_PREFIXES = ["task:", "tag:", "column:", "board:", "attachment:"]`) vs the clipboard `applies_to` set (which also includes `actor`, `project`) — The scope-chain branch of `focused_entity_type` only resolves a type for monikers whose prefix is in `INSPECTABLE_ENTITY_PREFIXES`, which omits `actor`/`project`. So a *palette* (scope-chain-only, no `ctx.target`) focus whose leaf is `actor:…`/`project:…` resolves to `None` and the gate SUPPRESSES the clipboard trio even though actor/project ARE clipboard-capable. Not a live regression today: actor/project are reached via context-menu (`ctx.target` wins verbatim and is unfiltered → resolves correctly, e.g. the `task_into_project` paste target), and they don't appear as bare scope-chain leaves in practice. Worth a note because the declared capability set (`applies_to`) and the resolver's known-type set (`INSPECTABLE_ENTITY_PREFIXES`) diverge — if actor/project ever become palette-focusable, clipboard would silently vanish for them. Consider documenting the intentional divergence or aligning the two sets.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/tests/list_applies_to.rs:64` — The fixture's `copyable` set (`["task", "tag", "column", "board", "attachment"]`) intentionally differs from the real declared set (missing `actor`, `project`). Fine for the isolated unit fixture, but a reader may mistake it for the canonical set. Consider a one-line comment that this is a deliberately reduced fixture, not the production list (which is pinned by `builtin_entity_commands_e2e::assert_clipboard_applies_to`).
+
+### Verification evidence (fresh, this review)
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-command-service` → 155 run: **154 passed, 1 failed**. The single failure is `meta_tree::meta_tree_id_param_is_required_where_expected` (`unregister.id required flag` assertion) — confirmed PRE-EXISTING by re-running it against a clean `git stash` of the working changes (still failed on HEAD), and unrelated to `applies_to` (no changed file touches the unregister id-required schema). This is the carded meta_tree failure, not a regression.
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-command-service --test list_applies_to` → **4/4 passed** (view-absent, perspective-absent, task-present, global-unchanged).
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-command-service entity_c` (clipboard `applies_to` metadata e2e) → **6/6 passed**, including `assert_clipboard_applies_to` pinning the declared set and excluding view/perspective.
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-kanban clipboard` → **50/50 passed**.
+- [ ] Red-green probe: neutered the gate (`applies_to_focus(None, req)` in `list_filter_matches`) → `list_applies_to` went **RED** on exactly the two suppression tests (view + perspective leaked) while task-present/global stayed green; restored the original line → **4/4 green** again. `service.rs` diff back to +49/-2 (original change), probe fully reverted.
+- [ ] tsc: the SDK (`crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/sdk/commands.ts`) and builtin plugins have no standalone tsconfig — they are bundled/validated through the Rust e2e harness (the only tsconfigs are the two app UIs, which this change does not touch and which the constraints forbid compiling). The authoritative typecheck for the `commands.ts` + `entity-commands/index.ts` changes is `builtin_entity_commands_e2e` (green), which loads the plugin and asserts the `applies_to` field flows through. The kanban-app vitest suites (50/50 + 75/75 per the implementer) cover UI that this change does not modify (no `.tsx` files changed).
\ No newline at end of file

+Overall: the fix is well-designed and metadata-driven. The findings below are a data-drift warning and two nits — all addressed in the 2026-06-13 review-fix pass.

 ### Warnings
-- [ ] `builtin/plugins/entity-commands/index.ts:72` + `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/clipboard_commands.rs:30` — The clipboard capability set is duplicated as TWO independent source-of-truth lists: TS `CLIPBOARD_ENTITY_TYPES` (list-time gate) and Rust `COPYABLE_ENTITY_TYPES` (dispatch-time `available()` gate). They currently agree exactly (`task, tag, column, board, actor, project, attachment`), but lockstep is enforced only by prose comments ("Mirrors…/Must stay in sync…") plus a hardcoded literal in `tests/integration/builtin_entity_commands_e2e.rs::assert_clipboard_applies_to` (which pins the TS-declared set against a third hand-maintained literal, not against the Rust list). Declared ⟺ enforced can silently drift: e.g. if Rust later drops `actor` from `COPYABLE_ENTITY_TYPES`, the command would still LIST for a focused actor (TS unchanged) but then no-op/fail at dispatch — re-introducing a shown-but-unsupported command, the exact class of bug this card fixes. Suggested fix: add a single drift-guard test asserting the TS-surfaced `applies_to` equals the Rust `COPYABLE_ENTITY_TYPES` (the e2e harness already loads the plugin and has the registered metadata + the Rust constant in scope), so the two lists cannot diverge without a red test.
+- [x] **Declared ⟺ enforced drift guard.** `builtin/plugins/entity-commands/index.ts` `CLIPBOARD_ENTITY_TYPES` (list-time gate) and `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/clipboard_commands.rs` `COPYABLE_ENTITY_TYPES` (dispatch-time `available()` gate) were two independent source-of-truth lists, kept in lockstep only by prose comments plus a third hand-maintained literal in the e2e. **FIXED (option a):** made `COPYABLE_ENTITY_TYPES` the canonical `pub` constant and rewrote `builtin_entity_commands_e2e::assert_clipboard_applies_to` to assert set-equality between the TS-surfaced `applies_to` (read through the real registered metadata via `list command`) and the Rust `COPYABLE_ENTITY_TYPES` directly — no third literal. The two lists can no longer diverge without a RED test. Red-green proven: dropping `actor` from the Rust constant turned the guard RED (`left` includes actor, `right` does not); restoring it → GREEN.

 ### Nits

So a *palette* (scope-chain-only, no `ctx.target`) focus whose leaf is `actor:…`/`project:…` resolves to `None` and the gate SUPPRESSES the clipboard trio even though actor/project ARE clipboard-capable. reverse_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -17,40 +17,47 @@
 - For entity types that DO support them (tasks/cards), they continue to appear and work.
 - Availability is computed from plugin/command metadata for the focused scope, not branched on entity-type strings in the UI.

+## Design / investigate
+
+- This is a metadata-driven-UI concern: the UI is an interpreter of command metadata for the focused scope chain, never hardcoding entity logic in React. Reuse the existing command-applicability/scope-resolution path that already decides which commands render for a focused entity (the same machinery behind `{{entity.type}}` caption rendering and per-scope command lists).
+- Find where cut/copy/paste commands are declared: likely entity.yaml / a clipboard or edit command YAML, plus the paste path (PasteMatrix — internal vs external drag distinction already exists). Determine why they leak onto view/perspective scopes — are they declared at a too-broad scope, or is the UI listing all commands regardless of applicability?
+- The fix is to have the plugin/command declaration express which entity types support cut/copy/paste (capability/applicability), and the command surface filter by that — so unsupported entities never list them.
+- Confirm copy/cut/paste are genuinely unsupported for views & perspectives (the user says it's OK that they're unsupported — the ask is purely to stop showing them).
+
 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [x] Cut/copy/paste do not appear on the command surface when a view or perspective is focused
-- [x] Cut/copy/paste still appear and work for entity types that support them (tasks/cards)
-- [x] Availability is resolved from plugin/command metadata for the focused scope — no hardcoded entity-type branch in React
-- [x] No regression to internal-drag (task.move) vs external-drag (paste) dispatch separation
+- [ ] Cut/copy/paste do not appear on the command surface when a view or perspective is focused
+- [ ] Cut/copy/paste still appear and work for entity types that support them (tasks/cards)
+- [ ] Availability is resolved from plugin/command metadata for the focused scope — no hardcoded entity-type branch in React
+- [ ] No regression to internal-drag (task.move) vs external-drag (paste) dispatch separation

 ## Tests
-- [x] vitest red-first: with a view/perspective focused, the command surface (palette/menu) does NOT include cut/copy/paste
-- [x] vitest: with a task/card focused, cut/copy/paste ARE present
-- [x] command-metadata/applicability test proving the gate is driven by declared capability, not a UI string check
-- [x] tsc + touched vitest green; relevant cargo nextest packages green if command YAML/declaration changes touch a Rust crate
+- [ ] vitest red-first: with a view/perspective focused, the command surface (palette/menu) does NOT include cut/copy/paste
+- [ ] vitest: with a task/card focused, cut/copy/paste ARE present
+- [ ] command-metadata/applicability test proving the gate is driven by declared capability, not a UI string check
+- [ ] tsc + touched vitest green; relevant cargo nextest packages green if command YAML/declaration changes touch a Rust crate
+
+## Constraints
+- NO whole-workspace cargo build/clippy. Never touch .kanban/actors/wballard.jsonl.
+- Metadata-driven only — do not hardcode view/perspective special-casing in React (see metadata-driven-ui feedback). Cross-cutting commands live once in entity.yaml/ui.yaml; type-specific commands live in their noun's YAML — gate via applicability, don't re-list.
+
+## Workflow
+- /tdd — failing test first: focused view/perspective excludes cut/copy/paste from the command surface.

 ## Review Findings (2026-06-13 14:53)

-Overall: the fix is well-designed and metadata-driven. The findings below are a data-drift warning and two nits — all addressed in the 2026-06-13 review-fix pass.
+Overall: the fix is well-designed and metadata-driven — the gate is a declared entity-type set (`applies_to`) interpreted by one code path (`CommandService::applies_to_focus` → `caption::focused_entity_type`), with NO hardcoded "view"/"perspective" branch in either Rust or React (verified by grep across `apps/kanban-app/ui/src`, `builtin/plugins/entity-commands`, `builtin/plugins/app-shell-commands`). `focused_entity_type` is now shared by caption rendering and the gate (two callers), so label and availability are decided by one rule. Tests are green and the red-green probe holds. No blockers. The findings below are a data-drift warning and two nits.

 ### Warnings
-- [x] **Declared ⟺ enforced drift guard.** `builtin/plugins/entity-commands/index.ts` `CLIPBOARD_ENTITY_TYPES` (list-time gate) and `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/clipboard_commands.rs` `COPYABLE_ENTITY_TYPES` (dispatch-time `available()` gate) were two independent source-of-truth lists, kept in lockstep only by prose comments plus a third hand-maintained literal in the e2e. **FIXED (option a):** made `COPYABLE_ENTITY_TYPES` the canonical `pub` constant and rewrote `builtin_entity_commands_e2e::assert_clipboard_applies_to` to assert set-equality between the TS-surfaced `applies_to` (read through the real registered metadata via `list command`) and the Rust `COPYABLE_ENTITY_TYPES` directly — no third literal. The two lists can no longer diverge without a RED test. Red-green proven: dropping `actor` from the Rust constant turned the guard RED (`left` includes actor, `right` does not); restoring it → GREEN.
+- [ ] `builtin/plugins/entity-commands/index.ts:72` + `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/clipboard_commands.rs:30` — The clipboard capability set is duplicated as TWO independent source-of-truth lists: TS `CLIPBOARD_ENTITY_TYPES` (list-time gate) and Rust `COPYABLE_ENTITY_TYPES` (dispatch-time `available()` gate). They currently agree exactly (`task, tag, column, board, actor, project, attachment`), but lockstep is enforced only by prose comments ("Mirrors…/Must stay in sync…") plus a hardcoded literal in `tests/integration/builtin_entity_commands_e2e.rs::assert_clipboard_applies_to` (which pins the TS-declared set against a third hand-maintained literal, not against the Rust list). Declared ⟺ enforced can silently drift: e.g. if Rust later drops `actor` from `COPYABLE_ENTITY_TYPES`, the command would still LIST for a focused actor (TS unchanged) but then no-op/fail at dispatch — re-introducing a shown-but-unsupported command, the exact class of bug this card fixes. Suggested fix: add a single drift-guard test asserting the TS-surfaced `applies_to` equals the Rust `COPYABLE_ENTITY_TYPES` (the e2e harness already loads the plugin and has the registered metadata + the Rust constant in scope), so the two lists cannot diverge without a red test.

 ### Nits
-- [x] **`caption.rs` INSPECTABLE_ENTITY_PREFIXES vs clipboard set divergence.** Documented the intentional divergence: `INSPECTABLE_ENTITY_PREFIXES` is deliberately a subset of `COPYABLE_ENTITY_TYPES` (omits `actor`/`project`, which are reached only via explicit context-menu target, never as bare scope-chain leaves), with an explicit note that if actor/project ever become palette-focusable their prefixes must be added.
-- [x] **`list_applies_to.rs` reduced fixture.** Added a comment that the fixture's `copyable` set is a deliberately REDUCED fixture (missing actor/project), NOT the canonical production list, which is pinned against the Rust constant by `builtin_entity_commands_e2e::assert_clipboard_applies_to`.
